title: OISD Safety Standard 114 — Pressure Relief
content: OISD-STD-114: Hazardous area classification and pressure relief systems. Section 6.3: All centrifugal pump discharge lines operating above 10 bar MUST be fitted with an independent pressure safety valve. If the main inlet valve fails closed, causing dead-head conditions, the PSV must relieve within 30 seconds. Failure to maintain the PSV or bypass path constitutes a Class-A compliance violation subject to immediate shutdown order.

title: SOP-402: Emergency Pump Starvation Procedure
content:
Standard Operating Procedure #402 — Emergency Response for Pump Starvation Events.

Scope: Applies to all centrifugal pumps in Unit 100.

Step 1: If main feed valve V-105 fails closed or is inadvertently shut, IMMEDIATELY open bypass valve V-106 to maintain minimum flow to Pump P-201.
Step 2: Monitor pump discharge pressure. If pressure drops below 8.0 bar, initiate controlled shutdown of P-201.
Step 3: Notify Shift Supervisor and issue emergency maintenance ticket for V-105.
Step 4: Do NOT restart P-201 until flow path is confirmed via V-105 or V-106.

Critical Note: Cavitation onset for P-201 occurs within 3 minutes of total flow loss. Mechanical seal failure follows within 8 minutes.

title: OEM Manual — Kirloskar KPD+ Centrifugal Pump
content:
Kirloskar KPD+ 65x40-250 Centrifugal Pump — Operation & Maintenance Manual.

Section 7.2 Cavitation Warning: Cavitation begins when NPSH available drops below 3.2m. At zero flow (dead-head), cavitation onset is immediate. Impeller damage occurs within 3 minutes. Mechanical seal failure follows within 8 minutes.

Section 9.1 Bearing Maintenance: Replace bearings every 8000 operating hours or upon vibration exceeding 4.5 mm/s RMS.

Section 11.3 Troubleshooting: If pump exhibits unusual noise or vibration, check suction strainer for blockage, verify inlet valve is fully open, and confirm adequate NPSH.

title: Work Order WO-98422 — V-105 Actuator Issue
content:
Work Order #WO-98422
Date: 2024-11-20
Equipment: V-105 (Main Feed Valve)
Priority: High
Technician: R. Sharma

Problem: Valve actuator sticking intermittently during routine cycling test. Valve failed to fully open on 2 out of 5 test cycles.

Action Taken: Actuator lubricated and linkage adjusted. Successfully cycled 10 times post-repair.

Recommendation: MONITOR CLOSELY. If sticking recurs within 30 days, schedule full actuator replacement. Consider upgrading to electric actuator per MOC-2024-112.

Status: Closed — Monitoring

title: OISD Standard 117 — Storage Tank Safety
content: OISD-STD-117: Fire protection and safety for storage tanks. Section 4.2: All atmospheric storage tanks exceeding 500 kL capacity must have bund walls capable of containing 110% of the largest tank volume. Annual shell thickness measurements are mandatory.

title: Work Order WO-93800 — P-201 Seal Replacement
content:
Work Order #WO-93800
Date: 2024-03-20
Equipment: P-201 (Centrifugal Feed Pump)
Priority: High
Technician: R. Sharma

Problem: Minor drip detected at mechanical seal during routine inspection. Seal was original (5 years in service).

Action Taken: Replaced mechanical seal assembly (John Crane Type 2100). Pump realigned using laser alignment tool. Post-repair vibration: 2.1 mm/s (within spec).

Root Cause: Normal wear after 5 years of continuous service.

Status: Closed

title: SOP-115: Valve Isolation & LOTO Procedure
content:
Standard Operating Procedure #115 — Valve Isolation and Lock-Out/Tag-Out.

Scope: All manual and automated valves in Units 100-500.

Before isolating any valve for maintenance:
1. Obtain Permit to Work from Control Room.
2. Confirm zero-energy state downstream.
3. Apply LOTO lock and personal danger tag.
4. Verify isolation using local pressure gauge.

Critical: Double-block-and-bleed configuration required for any valve rated above 10 bar.

title: OEM Manual — Alfa Laval Heat Exchanger
content:
Alfa Laval Aalborg OC-250 Shell & Tube Heat Exchanger — Technical Manual.

Section 3.1: Maximum design temperature 200°C, maximum design pressure 20 bar.
Section 5.2: Clean tube-side every 12 months or when ΔT across exchanger drops below design by more than 15%.
Section 6.1 Emergency: If cooling flow is lost, outlet temperature will rise at approximately 8°C per minute. Initiate controlled shutdown if outlet exceeds 160°C.

title: Incident Report IR-2023-047
content:
Incident Report IR-2023-047
Date: 2023-07-14
Location: Unit 100 - Product Tank Farm
Severity: Near Miss

Description: During night shift, level transmitter on T-501 gave erroneous high reading due to condensation in impulse line. Operator nearly shut product inlet valve, which would have caused reactor pressure excursion.

Root Cause: Inadequate heat tracing on impulse line during monsoon season.

Corrective Action: Heat tracing installed on all impulse lines. Redundant level measurement added (radar type). Operator training updated.

Lesson Learned: Single-point instrument failures in safety-critical services must be addressed with redundancy.
